title: Sarga 14
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Then, accompanied by six powerful ministers, including Mahodara, Prahasta, Maricha, Shuka, and Sarana, he proceeded.” through “Then, the commotion of the armies of that king of Rākṣasas (Ravana) grew like the great ocean, as if shaking a mountain.” through “There, Dashanana, being struck down as if breathless, was obstructed by the continuous onslaught of weapons, much like clouds pouring down…”

Themes: devotion, mind and meditation, mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “Even though struck by the weapons of the Yakshas, he felt no pain, just like a mountain remains unaffected when drenched by hundreds of streams…” through “There was no space left in the sky due to the multitude of those killed going to heaven, those fighting on earth, and the groups of sages…” through “Then, he entered the archway, which was the boundary of the doorkeepers, adorned with gold and sprinkled with sapphire dust.”

The passage moves from “O King, the doorkeeper known as Suryabhanu obstructed that Dashagriva, the night-wanderer, as he was entering.” through “Though struck by the archway resembling a mountain peak, the hero suffered no harm due to the boon granted by Svayambhu (Brahma).” through “Then, all of them fled, having witnessed the Rakshasa's prowess, and, afflicted by fear, exhausted, with pale faces, and having abandoned their…”

verses.sanskrit: ततस्तु सचिवैः सार्धं षड्भिर्नित्यं बलोद्धतैः । महोदरप्रहस्ताभ्यां मारीचशुकसारणैः ।। 7.14.1 ।।
verses.translation: Then, accompanied by six powerful ministers, including Mahodara, Prahasta, Maricha, Shuka, and Sarana, he proceeded.

verses.sanskrit: धूम्राक्षेण च वीरेण नित्यं समरगृध्नुना । वृतः संप्रययौ श्रीमान्क्रोधाल्लोकान्दहन्निव ।। 7.14.2 ।।
verses.translation: Accompanied by the brave Dhūmrākṣa, who was always eager for battle, the glorious Ravana set out, as if burning the worlds with his rage.

verses.sanskrit: पुराणि स नदीः शैलान्वनान्युपवनानि च । अतिक्रम्य मुहूर्तेन कैलासं गिरिमागमत् ।। 7.14.3 ।।
verses.translation: He swiftly crossed cities, rivers, mountains, forests, and groves, reaching Mount Kailasa in a short time.

verses.sanskrit: सन्निविष्टं गिरौ तस्मिन्राक्षसेन्द्रं निशम्य तु । युद्धे ऽत्यर्थकृतोत्साहं दुरात्मानं समन्त्रिणम् ।। 7.14.4 ।।
verses.translation: The lord of Rakshasas, Ravana, was perceived to be settled on that mountain with his ministers, an evil-minded one extremely enthusiastic for battle.

verses.sanskrit: यक्षा न शेकुः संस्थातुं प्रमुखे तस्य रक्षसः । राज्ञो भ्रातेति विज्ञाय गता यत्र धनेश्वरः ।। 7.14.5 ।।
verses.translation: The Yakṣas were unable to stand before that Rākṣasa (Ravana), and knowing him to be the king's (Kubera's) brother, they went to where Kubera, the lord of wealth, was.

verses.sanskrit: ते गत्वा सर्वमाचख्युर्भ्रातुस्तस्य चिकीर्षितम् । अनुज्ञाता ययुर्हष्टा युद्धाय धनदेन ते ।। 7.14.6 ।।
verses.translation: Having gone, they reported all of his brother's intentions, and being permitted by Kubera, they joyfully went forth to battle.

verses.sanskrit: ततो बलानां सङ्क्षोभो व्यवर्धत महोदधेः । तस्य नैर्ऋतराजस्य शैलं सञ्चालयन्निव ।। 7.14.7 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the commotion of the armies of that king of Rākṣasas (Ravana) grew like the great ocean, as if shaking a mountain.

verses.sanskrit: ततो युद्धं समभवद्यक्षराक्षससङ्कुलम् । व्यथिताश्चाभवंस्तत्र सचिवा राक्षसस्य ते ।। 7.14.8 ।।
verses.translation: Then a battle ensued, crowded with Yakṣas and Rākṣasas, and there the ministers of the Rākṣasa (Ravana) became distressed.

verses.sanskrit: स दृष्ट्वा तादृशं सैन्यं दशग्रीवो निशाचरः । हर्षनादान्बहून्कृत्वा स क्रोधादभ्यधावत ।। 7.14.9 ।।
verses.translation: Seeing such a vast army, the demon Dashagriva, after uttering many roars of joy, rushed forward in a fit of rage.

verses.sanskrit: ये तु ते राक्षसेन्द्रस्य सचिवा घोरविक्रमाः । तेषां सहस्रमेकैकं यक्षाणां समयोधयन् ।। 7.14.10 ।।
verses.translation: Each of those ministers of the king of demons, who were of terrible prowess, fought against a thousand Yakshas.

verses.sanskrit: ततो गदाभिर्मुसलैरसिभिः शक्तितोमरैः । हन्यमानो दशग्रीवस्तत्सैन्यं समगाहत ।। 7.14.11 ।।
verses.translation: Then, even while being struck by maces, clubs, swords, spears, and javelins, Dashagriva plunged into that army.

verses.sanskrit: स निरुच्छ्वासवत्तत्र वध्यमानो दशाननः । वर्षद्भिरिव जीमूतैर्धाराभिरवरुध्यत ।। 7.14.12 ।।
verses.translation: There, Dashanana, being struck down as if breathless, was obstructed by the continuous onslaught of weapons, much like clouds pouring down streams of rain.

verses.sanskrit: न चकार व्यथां चैव यक्षशस्त्रैः समाहतः । महीधर इवाम्भोदैर्धाराशतसमुक्षितः ।। 7.14.13 ।।
verses.translation: Even though struck by the weapons of the Yakshas, he felt no pain, just like a mountain remains unaffected when drenched by hundreds of streams of rain from clouds.

verses.sanskrit: स दुरात्मा समुद्यम्य कालदण्डोपमां गदाम् । प्रविवेश ततः सैन्यं नयन्यक्षान्यमक्षयम् ।। 7.14.14 ।।
verses.translation: The evil-minded one, having raised his mace resembling the staff of Yama, then entered the army, leading the Yakshas to their death.

verses.sanskrit: स कक्षमिव विस्तीर्णं शुष्केन्धनमिवाकुलम् । वातेनाग्निरिवायत्तो यक्षसैन्यं ददाह तत् ।। 7.14.15 ।।
verses.translation: He, like a fire empowered by the wind, burned that vast Yaksha army, which was crowded like a dry forest filled with dry fuel.

verses.sanskrit: तैस्तु तत्र महामात्यैर्महोदरशुकादिभिः । अल्पावशेषास्ते यक्षाः कृता वातैरिवाम्बुदाः ।। 7.14.16 ।।
verses.translation: There, those Yakshas were reduced to a few remaining, like clouds scattered by winds, by those great ministers such as Mahodara and Shuka.

verses.sanskrit: केचित्समाहता भग्नाः पतिताः समरक्षितौ । ओष्ठांश्च दशनैस्तीक्ष्णैरदशन्कुपिता रणे ।। 7.14.17 ।।
verses.translation: Some, struck down and defeated, fell on the battlefield, while others, enraged in battle, bit their lips with sharp teeth.

verses.sanskrit: श्रान्ताश्चान्योन्यमालिङ्ग्य भ्रष्टशस्त्रा रणाजिरे । सीदन्ति च तदा यक्षाः कूला इव जलेन ह ।। 7.14.18 ।।
verses.translation: Exhausted and with their weapons lost, the Yakshas then collapsed on the battlefield, embracing each other, like riverbanks eroded by water.

verses.sanskrit: हतानां गच्छतां स्वर्गं युध्यतां पृथिवीतले । प्रेक्षतामृषिसङ्घानां न बभूवान्तरं दिवि ।। 7.14.19 ।।
verses.translation: There was no space left in the sky due to the multitude of those killed going to heaven, those fighting on earth, and the groups of sages watching.

verses.sanskrit: भग्नांस्तु तान्समालक्ष्य यक्षेन्द्रांस्तु महाबलान् । धनाध्यक्षो महाबाहुः प्रेषयामास यक्षकान् ।। 7.14.20 ।।
verses.translation: Seeing those powerful Yaksha chiefs defeated, the mighty-armed Kubera, the lord of wealth, dispatched other Yakshas.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे राम विस्तीर्णबलवाहनः । प्रेषितो न्यपतद्यक्षो नाम्ना संयोधकण्टकः ।। 7.14.21 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, at that very moment, a Yaksha named Samyodhakantaka, sent with a vast army and chariots, attacked.

verses.sanskrit: तेन चक्रेण मारीचो विष्णुनेव रणे हतः । पतितो भूतले शैलात्क्षीणपुण्य इव ग्रहः ।। 7.14.22 ।।
verses.translation: Maricha was struck down in battle by that discus, as if by Vishnu, and fell to the earth like a planet whose merits are exhausted falling from a mountain.

verses.sanskrit: ससञ्ज्ञस्तु मुहूर्तेन स विश्रम्य निशाचरः । तं यक्षं योधयामास स च भग्नः प्रदुद्रुवे ।। 7.14.23 ।।
verses.translation: However, the night-wanderer Maricha regained consciousness in a moment, rested, and then fought that Yaksha, who was subsequently defeated and fled.

verses.sanskrit: ततः काञ्चनचित्राङ्गं वैडूर्यरजसोक्षितम् । मर्यादां प्रतिहाराणां तोरणान्तरमाविशत् ।। 7.14.24 ।।
verses.translation: Then, he entered the archway, which was the boundary of the doorkeepers, adorned with gold and sprinkled with sapphire dust.

verses.sanskrit: तं तु राजन्दशग्रीवं प्रविशन्तं निशाचरम् । सूर्यभानुरिति ख्यातो द्वारपालो न्यवारयत् ।। 7.14.25 ।।
verses.translation: O King, the doorkeeper known as Suryabhanu obstructed that Dashagriva, the night-wanderer, as he was entering.

verses.sanskrit: स वार्यमाणो यक्षेण प्रविवेश निशाचरः । यदा तु वारितो राम न व्यतिष्ठत्स राक्षसः ।। 7.14.26 ।।
verses.translation: Though obstructed by the Yaksha, the night-wanderer entered, and O Rama, when that demon was stopped, he did not halt.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्तोरणमुत्पाट्य तेन यक्षेण ताडितः । रुधिरं प्रस्रवन्भाति शैलो धातुस्रवैरिव ।। 7.14.27 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having uprooted the archway, he was struck by that Yaksha, and with blood flowing, he appeared like a mountain with streams of minerals.

verses.sanskrit: स शैलशिखराभेण तोरणेन समाहतः । जगाम न क्षतिं वीरो वरदानात्स्वयम्भुवः ।। 7.14.28 ।।
verses.translation: Though struck by the archway resembling a mountain peak, the hero suffered no harm due to the boon granted by Svayambhu (Brahma).

verses.sanskrit: तेनैव तोरणेनाथ यक्षस्तेनाभिताडितः । नादृश्यत तदा यक्षो भस्मीकृततनुस्तदा ।। 7.14.29 ।।
verses.translation: The Yaksha, struck by Ravana with that very archway, was then not seen, his body having been reduced to ashes.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रदुद्रुवुः सर्वे दृष्ट्वा रक्षःपराक्रमम् । ततो नदीर्गुहाश्चैव विविशुर्भयपीडिताः । त्यक्तप्रहरणाः श्रान्ता विवर्णवदनास्तदा ।। 7.14.30 ।।
verses.translation: Then, all of them fled, having witnessed the Rakshasa's prowess, and, afflicted by fear, exhausted, with pale faces, and having abandoned their weapons, they then entered rivers and caves. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे चतुर्दशः सर्गः ।। 14 ।। Thus ends the fourteenth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
